Title: The Transcription Factors NFYA1 and GBF3 Jointly Regulate CHS2 to Promote Tangeretin Accumulation and Cold Tolerance in Citrus

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chalcone synthase; type III polyketide synthase
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies core flavonoid scaffold required for target product biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form the chalcone scaffold that feeds tangeretin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant/ROS scavenging; enhances cold tolerance; nutraceutical value; antifungal activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tangeretin accumulation and enhances cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CiCHS2 was the most cold-induced CHS gene in Ichang papeda. 2. VIGS and CRISPR/Cas9 loss of CiCHS2 reduced CHS activity, tangeretin content, and cold tolerance. 3. Exogenous tangeretin partially/comparably rescued cold-sensitive phenotypes of CiCHS2-silenced plants. 4. Heterologous overexpression of CiCHS2 in tobacco increased CHS activity, tangeretin content, and cold tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression comparison; RT-qPCR; promoter-GUS cold induction assay; subcellular localization; VIGS; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; heterologous overexpression in tobacco; CHS activity assay; metabolite profiling of tangeretin; cold stress phenotyping; tangeretin rescue assay
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key/rate-limiting enzyme for flavonoid synthesis and key mediator of cold-inducible tangeretin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression/localization; native CiCHS2 promoter for GUS/LUC reporters
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by cold in leaves of Ichang papeda
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In citrus leaves induced by cold; in transgenic tobacco constitutively overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold treatment (4°C, -4°C, or 2°C then -2°C depending assay); exogenous tangeretin pretreatment (500 μM)
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CiNFYA1; CiGBF3 (transcriptional regulators of its promoter)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (published earlier study; accession NA); metabolomic dataset referenced in Figure S1 (accession NA)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological difference under normal growth conditions reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising metabolic engineering target to enhance cold tolerance via increasing tangeretin; could be stacked with upstream regulators CiNFYA1 and CiGBF3
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper frames CiCHS2 as the major cold-responsive CHS among four annotated CHS genes; downstream O-methyltransferases may also contribute to tangeretin biosynthesis but were not core validated here

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP transcription factor; G-box binding factor 3
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant/ROS scavenging; enhances cold tolerance; nutraceutical value
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of tangeretin biosynthesis via CHS2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CiCHS2 by binding the G-box element in the CiCHS2 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tangeretin accumulation and enhances cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive upstream activator of cold-inducible CiCHS2-dependent tangeretin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CiGBF3 was identified by Y1H as a CiCHS2 promoter-binding TF. 2. It directly bound the G-box in the CiCHS2 promoter and activated reporter expression. 3. Overexpression in tobacco increased CHS activity, tangeretin accumulation, and cold tolerance. 4. VIGS in Citrus ichangensis reduced CiCHS2 expression, CHS activity, tangeretin content, and cold tolerance. 5. Exogenous tangeretin rescued the cold-sensitive phenotype of CiGBF3-silenced plants.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-LUC transactivation; subcellular localization; transcription activation assay; overexpression in tobacco; VIGS in citrus; CHS activity assay; tangeretin measurement; cold stress phenotyping; tangeretin rescue assay
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a protein complex/heterodimer with CiNFYA1 that enhances activation of CiCHS2
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cold stress; transcriptional activation by CiNFYA1; ABA inducible (reported in discussion/Fig. S20)
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of a hierarchical feedforward-like module where CiNFYA1 activates CiGBF3 and both activate CiCHS2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Progressively induced by cold in Ichang papeda
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In citrus leaves induced by cold; in transgenic tobacco constitutively overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression/localization; native CiCHS2 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold treatment; exogenous tangeretin pretreatment in rescue assays; ABA inducibility noted
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious phenotype under normal growth conditions reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream engineering target for improving cold tolerance through boosting endogenous tangeretin biosynthesis; suitable for stacking with CiNFYA1/CiCHS2
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CiGBF3 did not appear to regulate CiNFYA1 transcription based on VIGS expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Y1H cDNA library screen from C. ichangensis; RNA-seq expression context from prior study (accession NA)

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NF-YA transcription factor; nuclear transcription factor Y subunit A
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant/ROS scavenging; enhances cold tolerance; nutraceutical value
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of tangeretin biosynthesis via direct activation of CiCHS2 and upstream activation of CiGBF3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CiCHS2 by binding the CCAAT element in its promoter; activates CiGBF3 by binding CCAAT motifs in the CiGBF3 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes CiCHS2-mediated tangeretin accumulation and enhances cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Hierarchical upstream activator that both directly activates CiCHS2 and amplifies the pathway through CiGBF3
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CiNFYA1 was identified by Y1H as a CiCHS2 promoter-binding TF. 2. It directly bound the CCAAT element in the CiCHS2 promoter and activated reporter expression. 3. VIGS of CiNFYA1 reduced CiCHS2 expression, CHS activity, tangeretin content, and cold tolerance. 4. CiNFYA1 directly activated CiGBF3 transcription by binding its promoter. 5. CiNFYA1 physically interacted with CiGBF3, and the complex synergistically enhanced CiCHS2 promoter activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-LUC transactivation; subcellular localization; transcription activation assay; VIGS in citrus; expression analysis of downstream genes; BiFC; LCI; CHS activity assay; tangeretin measurement; cold stress phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Physically interacts with CiGBF3 to form a transcriptionally cooperative complex on CiCHS2 regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cold stress; ABA inducible (reported in discussion/Fig. S20)
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts in a hierarchical feedforward-like cascade by activating CiGBF3 while also directly activating CiCHS2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by cold in Ichang papeda
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for localization/ChIP constructs; native CiCHS2 and CiGBF3 promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious phenotype under normal growth conditions reported for VIGS plants before stress
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate regulator for engineering cold tolerance through coordinated activation of CiGBF3 and CiCHS2-dependent tangeretin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No overexpression transgenic line phenotype was reported for CiNFYA1 in this paper; evidence is from binding, transactivation, interaction, and VIGS loss-of-function
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Y1H cDNA library screen from C. ichangensis; RNA-seq expression context from prior study (accession NA)
